title: Video shows Sullivan’s Tap patrons running for cover when shots fired near TD Garden
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Gunshots were heard near TD Garden around 10:45 p.m., prompting patrons at Sullivan's Tap to seek shelter.
Shots damaged the front door of Hotel Indigo and a vehicle near 100 Legends Way; no injuries reported.
Boston Police recovered ballistic evidence at the scene and the investigation is ongoing with no arrests made.

title: Wu cancels Harvard speaking engagement rather than cross grad-student picket line
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu cancels her Harvard Law School speaking engagement to avoid crossing a graduate student picket line.
The Harvard Graduate Students Union has been on strike since April 21 due to stalled contract negotiations.
Wu offered virtual participation but respected the union's decision, lauding her support for organized labor.

title: Mike’s Pastry is coming to Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Mike's Pastry will open a location in Logan Airport's Terminal C later this year, offering travelers signature favorites.
The menu will include filled-to-order cannoli, cheesecake slices, lobster tails, and a full espresso bar.
Mike's Pastry at Logan will operate daily from 5 a.m. to 10 p.m. in a blue, white, and gold space.

title: ‘Stranger Things’ actor Joe Keery joins star-studded cast for Broadway debut
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Joe Keery will make his Broadway debut alongside Julia Louis-Dreyfus in 'Other Desert Cities' starting Sept. 29, 2026.
The play, a Tony Award-winning work by Jon Robin Baitz, features a cast including Ed Harris, Allison Janney, and Lily Rabe.
'Other Desert Cities' will run for 16 weeks at the Hudson Theatre, with previews beginning September 2026.

title: Police close South Boston beach after ‘crowds of youth’ break into fights
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Police closed Carson Beach parking lot and part of Day Boulevard following youth fights in South Boston.
State police dispersed crowds from Carson Beach and Castle Island due to fights, loud music, and alcohol use.
The situation was cleared by 9:30 p.m. with no arrests or injuries reported, according to authorities.

title: Clover says it’s closing for good
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Clover Food Lab will close all 11 restaurants and meal-box delivery by May 28 after failing to find a buyer.
The vegetarian chain faced rising costs, intense competition, and limited pricing power, leading to unsustainable operating conditions.
Clover began as a food truck in 2008 and grew into a chain promoting local produce, before filing for bankruptcy in 2023.

title: Here are the local Mass. ties on the U.S. World Cup roster
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Matt Turner, a former New England Revolution goalkeeper, is included in the U.S. World Cup squad.
Harvard alumnus Matt Freese, now with New York City FC, is a goalkeeper on the U.S. roster.
Miles Robinson, an FC Cincinnati center-back from Arlington, is part of the U.S. World Cup team.

title: Stoneham police track down owner of enormous, nightmarish puppet
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Stoneham police tracked down the owner of a 7-foot, animatronic puppet found on a resident's lawn.
The puppet, identified as 'Bobby Strings' from Spirit Halloween, is temporarily held by police awaiting owner's consent for disposal.
Police used social media to locate the owner and speculated the puppet was discarded and left on the lawn as a prank.

title: Is it a good time to refinance your mortgage in Massachusetts? Well, it depends.
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A LendingTree report suggests Massachusetts homeowners could save $2,320 annually by refinancing 2023-2025 fixed-rate mortgages.
Current average 30-year mortgage rates have risen to 6.51%, driven by higher inflation and geopolitical tensions.
Borrowers with adjustable-rate mortgages or loans taken during COVID may find new refinancing opportunities despite higher fixed rates.

title: Rachael Rollins confirms she is running for old Suffolk DA job
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Rachael Rollins confirmed her candidacy for Suffolk District Attorney, submitting 1,606 certified nomination signatures.
Rollins resigned as U.S. attorney in 2023 amid ethical violations, including alleged election interference.
Current DA Kevin Hayden and candidate Linda Champion also submitted enough signatures for the election race.
